    • 浮血飘舟 - Means the blood floated on the river, indicating the severity of the violence.
    • 张轨拥众一方，威恩共著 - States that Zhang Gui, a local leader, maintained both authority and benevolence.
    • 凉州张骏字公彦 - Introduces Zhang Jun, a notable figure in Liangzhou.
    • 九年天雨五谷于武威，炖煌，植之悉生，因名天麦 - Describes a miraculous event where grains rained down in Wuwei and Dunhuang, and they all grew, leading to the name 'Heavenly Wheat.'
    • 凉州张祚伪和平中有神见于玄武殿，自称玄冥，与人言语 - Describes a divine figure appearing in the Xuanwu Hall, identifying himself as Xuanming and speaking with people.
    • 祚日夜祈之，神言与之福利，祚甚信之 - States that Zhang Zuo prayed to the divine figure daily, and the figure promised him good fortune, which Zhang Zuo believed.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 刘曜 - Liu Yao, a ruler of the Former Zhao dynasty.
    • 管涔之山 - Guan Cen Mountains, a specific mountain range in Shanxi Province.
    • 赵皇帝 - Zhao Emperor, referring to the ruler of the Zhao dynasty.
    • 石勒 - Shi Le, a founder of the Later Zhao dynasty.
    • 郭敬 - Guo Jing, a historical figure.
    • 襄国 - Xiangguo, a city in modern-day Xiangfen, Shanxi.
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang, a major city in Henan Province.
    • 大河 - The Yellow River.
    • 灵昌津 - Lingchang Ferry, a crossing point on the Yellow River.
    • 长安 - Chang'an, the capital of the Jin Dynasty.
    • 秦川 - Qin Plain, a region in Shaanxi Province.
    • 凉州 - Liangzhou, a region in modern-day Gansu Province.
    • 张轨 - Zhang Gui, a local leader in Liangzhou.
    • 张骏 - Zhang Jun, a notable figure in Liangzhou.
    • 武威 - Wuwei, a city in Gansu Province.
    • 炖煌 - Dunhuang, a city in Gansu Province.
    • 张祚 - Zhang Zuo, a ruler of Liangzhou.
    • 玄武殿 - Xuanwu Hall, a hall in the palace.
    • 玄冥 - Xuanming, a divine figure.

    Proper Nouns

    • 刘曜 - Liu Yao
    • 管涔之山 - Guan Cen Mountains
    • 赵皇帝 - Zhao Emperor
    • 石勒 - Shi Le
    • 郭敬 - Guo Jing
    • 襄国 - Xiangguo
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang
    • 大河 - The Yellow River
    • 灵昌津 - Lingchang Ferry
    • 长安 - Chang'an
    • 秦川 - Qin Plain
    • 凉州 - Liangzhou
    • 张轨 - Zhang Gui
    • 张骏 - Zhang Jun
    • 武威 - Wuwei
    • 炖煌 - Dunhuang
    • 张祚 - Zhang Zuo
    • 玄武殿 - Xuanwu Hall
    • 玄冥 - Xuanming

    Liu Yao lived in seclusion in the Guan Cen Mountains. One night, a child suddenly appeared and knelt down, saying, "The King of Guan Cen Mountain has sent me to pay respects to the Zhao Emperor, and to present a sword." He placed the sword before the emperor, bowed twice, and then left. When examined with a candle, the sword was two feet long, with an extraordinary shine, adorned with red jade, and had an inscription on its back: "Divine Sword for Wearing and Removing All Poisons."

    When Shi Le was a guest of Guo Jing, there was a prophecy in Xiangguo that said, "Strength is on the left, change is on the right, let go without words, or it enters the mouth." The prophecy explained that "let" minus "speech" becomes "Xiang," and "or" plus "mouth" becomes "guo" (country). Later, Shi Le established his capital in Xiangguo.

    Shi Le attacked Liu Yao in Luoyang, crossing the Yellow River from the south. At the time, the river was frozen, and just as the army was about to arrive, the ice began to thaw. The boats had no obstacles, and Shi Le captured Liu Yao alive, attributing the success to divine assistance. He named the crossing point "Lingchang Ferry."

    During the Jin Dynasty, a rhyme in Chang'an said, "The streets of Qin Plain were covered with blood, only Liangzhou stood calmly." During the reigns of Emperors Huizong and Min, the interior of the passes was devastated, with blood floating on the river. Zhang Gui, a local leader in Liangzhou, maintained both authority and benevolence.

    Zhang Jun of Liangzhou, whose courtesy name was Gongyan, had a miraculous event occur in the ninth year when grains rained down in Wuwei and Dunhuang. All the grains grew, and it was named "Heavenly Wheat."

    In the false peace period of Zhang Zuo of Liangzhou, a divine figure appeared in the Xuanwu Hall, identifying himself as Xuanming and speaking with people. Zhang Zuo prayed to the divine figure daily, and the figure promised him good fortune, which Zhang Zuo firmly believed. [27]

    凉州张重华遣谢艾伐麻狄，引师出振武。夜有二枭鸣于牙中，艾曰：“枭者邀也，六博得枭者胜。今枭鸣牙中，克敌之兆。”果大破之。
    　　宋武帝裕字德舆，小字寄奴。微时伐荻新洲，见大蛇长数丈，射之伤。明日，复至洲里，闻有杵臼声，往视之，见童子数人皆青衣捣药，问其故，答曰：“我王为刘寄奴所射，合散傅之。”帝曰：“王神何不杀之？”答曰：“刘寄奴王者不死，不可杀。”帝叱之皆散，仍收药而返。
    　　临淄牛山下有女水，齐人谚曰：“世治则女水流，世乱则女水竭。”慕容超时乾涸弥载，及天兵薄伐，乃激洪流。
    　　秦世有谣曰：“秦始皇，何僵梁。开吾户，据吾床。饮吾酒，唾吾浆，飧吾饣卞，以为粮。张吾弓，射东墙。前至沙邱当灭亡。”始皇既坑儒焚典，乃发孔子墓，欲取诸经传。圹既启，于是悉如谣者之言。又言谣文刊在冢壁，政甚恶之，乃远沙邱而循别路，见一群小儿辇沙为阜，问云沙邱，从此得病。 [28]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [28]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 凉州 - Historical region in present-day Gansu, China.
    • 张重华 - Historical figure from the Former Liang dynasty.
    • 谢艾 - Historical military general.
    • 麻狄 - Likely a place or a tribal name.
    • 振武 - Likely a place name or a military title.
    • 枭 - A type of bird, often associated with bad omens in Chinese culture.
    • 六博 - An ancient Chinese board game.
    • 宋武帝 - Emperor Wu of Song, a historical figure.
    • 刘寄奴 - Reference to Emperor Wu of Song.
    • 临淄 - Historical city in present-day Shandong, China.
    • 牛山 - Mountain in present-day Shandong, China.
    • 女水 - River in present-day Shandong, China.
    • 慕容超 - Historical figure from the Southern Yan dynasty.
    • 沙邱 - Historical place name in present-day Hebei, China.
    • 秦始皇 - The first emperor of the Qin dynasty.
    • 孔子 - Confucius, a historical and cultural figure in Chinese history.
    • 经传 - Refers to the classical texts and commentaries in Chinese culture.

    Proper Nouns

    • 凉州 - Historical region in present-day Gansu, China.
    • 张重华 - Historical figure from the Former Liang dynasty.
    • 谢艾 - Historical military general.
    • 麻狄 - Likely a place or a tribal name.
    • 振武 - Likely a place name or a military title.
    • 枭 - A type of bird, often associated with bad omens in Chinese culture.
    • 六博 - An ancient Chinese board game.
    • 宋武帝 - Emperor Wu of Song, a historical figure.
    • 刘寄奴 - Reference to Emperor Wu of Song.
    • 临淄 - Historical city in present-day Shandong, China.
    • 牛山 - Mountain in present-day Shandong, China.
    • 女水 - River in present-day Shandong, China.
    • 慕容超 - Historical figure from the Southern Yan dynasty.
    • 沙邱 - Historical place name in present-day Hebei, China.
    • 秦始皇 - The first emperor of the Qin dynasty.
    • 孔子 - Confucius, a historical and cultural figure in Chinese history.
    • 经传 - Refers to the classical texts and commentaries in Chinese culture.

    Zhang Zhonghua of Liang State dispatched Xie Ai to campaign against Ma Di, leading his army out from Zhenwu. At night, two owls called in the military camp. Xie Ai said, "The owl signifies an invitation, and in the game of Liu Bo, the one who gets the owl wins. Now that the owls are calling in the camp, it is an omen of victory over the enemy." Indeed, they achieved a great victory.

    Emperor Wu of Song, whose given name was Yu and courtesy name De Yu, was also known as Jinnu when he was obscure. When cutting reeds on Xinzhou, he saw a large snake several zhang long, shot it, and wounded it. The next day, he returned to the island and heard the sound of a pestle and mortar. Upon investigation, he saw several children in blue clothes grinding medicine. He asked them why, and they replied, "Our king was shot by Liu Jinnu and is mixing and applying medicine to heal him." The emperor said, "Why didn't the king's spirit kill him?" They answered, "Liu Jinnu is a king who cannot die, and he cannot be killed." The emperor scolded them, and they all dispersed, but he still collected the medicine and returned.

    Under Mount Niuzhu in Linzi, there is a river called Nüshui. The people of Qi say, "In times of peace, Nüshui flows; in times of chaos, Nüshui dries up." During the reign of Murong Chao, the river had been dry for many years. When the heavenly troops attacked, it suddenly surged with a great flood.

    In the Qin dynasty, there was a saying: "Emperor Qin Shi Huang, how did you become so stiff and inflexible? Open my door, occupy my bed. Drink my wine, spit in my drink. Eat my food, use it as provisions. Draw my bow, shoot at the east wall. Before reaching Shaoqiu, you will perish." After Emperor Qin Shi Huang buried the Confucian scholars alive and burned the classics, he opened Confucius's tomb to take the various classics and commentaries. When the tomb was opened, everything happened exactly as the saying had foretold. It was also said that the saying was carved on the tomb wall, and the emperor greatly disliked it. He then avoided Shaoqiu and took an alternative route, where he saw a group of children carting sand to form a mound. They said it was Shaoqiu, and from that point on, he fell ill. [28]

    晋武帝太康五年五月，宣帝庙地陷裂，梁无故自折。凡宗庙所以承祖先嗣，永世不刊安居，摧陷是毁绝之祥也。
    　　晋惠帝时，人有得一鸟毛，长三丈，以示张华。华惨然叹曰：“所谓海凫毛也。此毛出则天下土崩矣。”果如其言。
    　　晋惠帝永康元年，帝纳皇后羊氏。后将入宫，衣中忽有火光，众咸怪之。自后蕃臣构兵，洛阳失御，后为刘曜所嫔。
    　　晋永嘉元年，车骑大将军、东瀛王司马腾字元迈，自并州迁镇邺。行次真定时，久积雪而当其门，前方十数步独液不积。腾怪而掘之，得玉马，高尺许，口齿皆缺。腾以为马者国姓，称吉祥焉。或谓马无齿则不得食。未几晋遂大乱，腾后为汲桑所杀。 [29]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [29]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 宗庙所以承祖先嗣 - Classical phrase indicating the temple's role in preserving the ancestral lineage.
    • 所谓海凫毛也 - Classical phrase referring to the 'sea duck's feather,' predicting the collapse of the world.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋武帝 - Emperor Wu of Jin, the founder of the Western Jin dynasty.
    • 宣帝 - Emperor Wen of Wei, a significant historical figure in the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 张华 - A famous scholar and statesman of the Western Jin dynasty, known for his erudition and political acumen.
    • 晋惠帝 - Emperor Huai of Jin, the second emperor of the Western Jin dynasty.
    • 羊氏 - Empress Yang, the wife of Emperor Huai of Jin.
    • 洛阳 - The capital city of the Western Jin dynasty, a major political and cultural center.
    • 刘曜 - A Xiongnu leader who later established the Former Zhao dynasty.
    • 晋永嘉元年 - The first year of the Yong Jia era during the reign of Emperor Huai of Jin.
    • 车骑大将军 - A high-ranking military title in the Western Jin dynasty.
    • 东瀛王 - Prince of Dongjing, a noble title.
    • 司马腾 - A prominent military leader and politician in the Western Jin dynasty.
    • 并州 - An ancient Chinese state located in present-day Shanxi province.
    • 邺 - The capital of the Cao Wei kingdom during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 真定 - A place name in present-day Hebei province.
    • 玉马 - A jade horse, a symbol of good fortune and power in Chinese culture.
    • 汲桑 - A rebel leader during the Jin dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晋武帝 - Emperor Wu of Jin
    • 宣帝 - Emperor Wen of Wei
    • 张华 - Zhang Hua
    • 晋惠帝 - Emperor Huai of Jin
    • 羊氏 - Empress Yang
    • 洛阳 - Luoyang
    • 刘曜 - Liu Yao
    • 晋永嘉元年 - Yong Jia 1st year
    • 车骑大将军 - General-in-Chief
    • 东瀛王 - Prince of Dongjing
    • 司马腾 - Sima Teng
    • 并州 - Bingzhou
    • 邺 - Yecheng
    • 真定 - Zhendingshi
    • 玉马 - Jade horse
    • 汲桑 - Jishang

    During the fifth month of the fifth year of the Tai Kang era of Emperor Wu of Jin, the ground beneath the temple of Emperor Wen of Wei suddenly cracked, and the beams broke without apparent cause. Temples, which symbolize the continuity of ancestral lineage, should be stable and enduring; their destruction or damage is a bad omen.

    During the reign of Emperor Huai of Jin, a man found a bird feather three zhang long and showed it to Zhang Hua. Zhang Hua, with a solemn expression, sighed and said, "This is the feather of the sea duck. Its appearance foretells the collapse of the world." Indeed, his words came true.

    In the first year of the Yong Kang era of Emperor Huai of Jin, the emperor married Empress Yang. As she was about to enter the palace, she suddenly saw fire in her clothes, which was unusual and strange. After this event, rebellious vassals started wars, the capital Luoyang lost its control, and Empress Yang eventually became the concubine of Liu Yao.

    In the first year of the Yong Jia era of Emperor Huai of Jin, General-in-Chief and Prince of Dongjing, Sima Teng, moved from Bingzhou to Yecheng. When his caravan passed through Zhendingshi, there was a long accumulation of snow in front of his residence, but several steps ahead, the snow melted. Sima Teng found this strange and dug to uncover a jade horse about one chi tall, with missing teeth and mouth. He believed that the horse symbolized his royal lineage and considered it a sign of good fortune. Some people thought that the horse without teeth would be unable to eat. Shortly after, the Jin dynasty fell into chaos, and Sima Teng was later killed by Jishang. [29]

    董养字仲道，陈留浚仪人。泰始初到洛下，不干禄求荣。永嘉中洛城东北角步广里中地陷，有二鹅出焉，其苍者飞去，白者不能飞。养闻叹曰：“昔周时所盟会狄泉即此地也。今有二鹅，苍者胡象，后胡当入洛；白者不能飞，此国讳也。”
    　　晋孝武太元末，帝每闻手巾箱中有鼓吹鼙角之音，于是请僧斋会。夜见一臂长三丈许、手长数尺来摸经案。是岁帝崩，天下大乱，晋室自此而衰。
    　　晋孝武太元末有谶曰“修起会稽”。其后卢修果从会稽叛。
    　　晋义熙十一年，京都火灾大行，吴界尤甚。火防甚峻，犹自不绝。时王弘守吴郡，昼坐厅视事，忽见天上有一赤物下，状如信幡，遥集南人家屋上，须臾火遂大发。弘知天为之灾，故不罪始火之家。识者如晋室微弱之象也。 [30]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [30]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 不干禄求荣 - Means 'not seeking official position or honor,' indicating a refusal of worldly ambitions.
    • 国讳 - Means 'taboo of the state,' referring to a symbolic warning or prohibition.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 董养 - Dong Yang, a historical figure known for his wisdom and foresight.
    • 泰始 - The era name of Emperor Wu of Jin.
    • 永嘉 - The era name of Emperor Huai of Jin, a time of political turmoil and natural disasters.
    • 狄泉 - An ancient meeting place of the Zhou dynasty, a symbol of unity and alliance.
    • 晋孝武 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 太元 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin, a period of political instability.
    • 晋义熙 - The era name of Emperor Wudi of Jin.
    • 王弘 - A prominent official of the Jin dynasty, governing Wu Prefecture at the time of the fire.
    • 吴郡 - A prefecture in the Jin dynasty.
    • 会稽 - A region in the Jin dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 董养 - Dong Yang, a historical figure.
    • 仲道 - His courtesy name.
    • 泰始 - The era name of Emperor Wu of Jin.
    • 永嘉 - The era name of Emperor Huai of Jin.
    • 狄泉 - An ancient meeting place of the Zhou dynasty.
    • 晋孝武 - Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 太元 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 晋义熙 - The era name of Emperor Wudi of Jin.
    • 王弘 - A prominent official of the Jin dynasty.
    • 吴郡 - A prefecture in the Jin dynasty.
    • 会稽 - A region in the Jin dynasty.

    Dong Yang, courtesy name Zhongdao, was from Junyi, Chenliu. At the beginning of the Tai Shi era, he arrived in Luoyang, refusing to seek official position or honor. During the Yongjia period, the ground in the northeastern corner of the city, specifically in the area of "Bu Guang Li," collapsed, and two geese emerged. The one with a blue/green color flew away, while the white one could not fly. Upon hearing this, Dong Yang remarked, "This is the same place where the Zhou dynasty once held their alliances and meetings. Now there are two geese: the blue one symbolizes the Xiongnu, who will later enter the capital; the white one, unable to fly, is a taboo of the state."

    At the end of Emperor Xiaowu's Taiyuan era, the emperor often heard the sound of drums, trumpets, and horns in his handkerchief box. He then invited monks for a vegetarian meal. One night, he saw an arm about three zhang long and a hand several chi long reaching out to touch the sutra table. That year, the emperor died, and the world fell into chaos; the Jin dynasty began to decline from that point.

    At the end of Emperor Xiaowu's Taiyuan era, there was a prophecy that said "repair and build Kuaiji." Later, Lu Xiu indeed rebelled from Kuaiji.

    In the eleventh year of Emperor Wudi's Yixi era, a major fire broke out in the capital, especially in the Wu region. The fire prevention measures were very strict, but the fire still did not stop. At that time, Wang Hong was governing Wu Prefecture, sitting in his hall during the day to handle affairs, when suddenly he saw a red object falling from the sky, shaped like a flag, landing on the roof of a house in the southern part of the city, and shortly after, the fire broke out. Wang Hong knew it was a disaster sent by heaven, so he did not punish the family that started the fire. Those who understood it saw it as a sign of the weakening of the Jin dynasty. [30]

    隆安初吴郡治下狗常夜吠，聚皋桥上。人家狗有限而吠声甚众，或有夜觇视之，见一狗有两三头者，皆前向乱呔。无几有孙恩之乱。
    　　晋海西公时有贵人会，因藏区，有一手间在众臂之中，修骨巨指，毛色粗黑，举坐咸惊。寻为桓大司马所杀。旧传藏抠令人生离，斯验深矣。
    　　苻坚建元十二年，高陵县民穿井得大龟三尺六寸，背文负八卦古字。坚命作石池养之，食以粟。后死，藏其骨于太庙。其夜庙丞高虏梦龟谓曰：我本出将归江南，遭时不遇，陨命秦庭。即有人梦中谓虏曰，龟三千六百岁而终，终必妖兴，亡国之征也。未几为谢玄破于淮淝，自缢新城浮图中。
    　　慕容出畋，见一老父曰：“此非猎所，王宜还也。”明晨复去，值有白兔，驰马射之，坠石而卒。
    　　西秦乞伏炽磐都长安，端门外有一井，人常宿汲水亭之下。而夜闻磕磕有声，惊起照视，瓮中如血，中有丹鱼，长可三寸，而有寸光。时东羌西虏共相攻伐，国寻灭亡。 [31]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [31]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 隆安初 - The first year of the Long'an era (397 CE) during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 吳郡治下 - Refers to the administrative area under the jurisdiction of Wu Prefecture.
    • 狗常夜吠 - Describes dogs barking frequently at night.
    • 聚皋橋上 - Indicates that the dogs gathered on a bridge named Gao Bridge.
    • 人家狗有限而吠聲甚眾 - Suggests that despite the limited number of dogs, their barking was numerous.
    • 或有夜觇视之 - Implies that someone observed the situation at night.
    • 見一狗有两三头者，皆前向乱呔 - Describes a dog with multiple heads, all facing forward and barking chaotically.
    • 无几有孙恩之乱 - Indicates that shortly after, there was a rebellion led by Sun En.
    • 晋海西公时 - Refers to the time of Emperor Xie of Jin, also known as Emperor He.
    • 贵人会 - Describes a gathering of important people.
    • 藏区 - Refers to a type of food or grain.
    • 有一手间在众臂之中 - Describes a hand-like object found among the arms.
    • 修骨巨指，毛色粗黑 - Describes a long bone with large fingers and coarse black fur.
    • 举坐咸惊 - Indicates that everyone present was startled.
    • 寻为桓大司马所杀 - Suggests that the person was soon killed by Marshal Huán.
    • 旧传藏抠令人生离 - Refers to an old belief that certain objects could cause separation.
    • 斯验深矣 - Indicates that this belief was deeply verified.
    • 苻坚建元十二年 - The twelfth year of Emperor Fu Jian's reign during the Former Qin dynasty (381 CE).
    • 高陵县民穿井得大龟三尺六寸 - Describes a large turtle found in a well in Gaoling County, measuring three feet and six inches.
    • 背文负八卦古字 - Indicates that the turtle had ancient characters representing the Eight Trigrams on its back.
    • 坚命作石池养之 - Suggests that Emperor Fu Jian ordered a stone pool to be built to keep the turtle.
    • 食以粟 - Indicates that the turtle was fed millet.
    • 后死，藏其骨于太庙 - Describes the turtle's death and the burial of its bones in the imperial ancestral temple.
    • 其夜庙丞高虏梦龟谓曰 - Indicates that the night after, the temple steward named Gao Lü dreamed that the turtle spoke to him.
    • 我本出将归江南，遭时不遇，陨命秦庭 - The turtle said it was originally destined to return to the south but met an unfortunate fate in the Qin court.
    • 即有人梦中谓虏曰 - Suggests that someone in the dream told Gao Lü.
    • 龟三千六百岁而终，终必妖兴，亡国之征也 - Indicates that the turtle, having lived for 3600 years, predicted that its death would bring disaster and the downfall of the state.
    • 未几为谢玄破于淮淝，自缢新城浮图中 - Describes how shortly after, Xie Xuan defeated the Qin forces at the Battle of Fei River, and the turtle's prediction came true.
    • 慕容出畋 - Refers to Murong Ren going hunting.
    • 见一老父曰 - Describes encountering an old man.
    • 此非猎所，王宜还也 - The old man advised Murong Ren that this was not a place for hunting and he should return.
    • 明晨复去，值有白兔 - Indicates that Murong Ren went hunting again the next morning and encountered a white rabbit.
    • 驰马射之，坠石而卒 - Describes how Murong Ren chased the rabbit, fell from his horse, and died.
    • 西秦乞伏炽磐都长安 - Refers to the Western Qin ruler Qifu Chikhan establishing his capital in Chang'an.
    • 端门外有一井 - Describes a well in front of the main gate.
    • 人常宿汲水亭之下 - Indicates that people often stayed overnight at the water pavilion to draw water.
    • 而夜闻磕磕有声 - Describes hearing a tapping sound at night.
    • 惊起照视，瓮中如血，中有丹鱼 - Describes the people being startled, lighting a lantern, and seeing red water in a jar with a red fish.
    • 长可三寸，而有寸光 - Indicates that the fish was about three inches long and had a one-inch glow.
    • 时东羌西虏共相攻伐 - Describes the Eastern Qiang and Western barbarians fighting each other.
    • 国寻灭亡 - Indicates that the country soon fell.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 隆安初 - The first year of the Long'an era (397 CE) during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 吳郡治下 - Wu Prefecture, an important administrative region during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 孫恩之亂 - A rebellion led by Sun En in 399 CE, which significantly impacted the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 晉海西公時 - The reign of Emperor Xie of Jin (362-372 CE), also known as Emperor He.
    • 貴人會 - A gathering of important people, likely a high-ranking social event.
    • 藏區 - A type of food or grain, possibly a reference to a specific dish or meal.
    • 苻堅建元十二年 - The twelfth year of Emperor Fu Jian's reign (381 CE) during the Former Qin dynasty.
    • 高陵縣 - Gaoling County, an administrative region during the Former Qin dynasty.
    • 苻堅 - Emperor Fu Jian (338-385 CE), the ruler of the Former Qin dynasty.
    • 桓大司馬 - Marshal Huán, a high-ranking military official during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 舊傳藏扣令人生離 - An old belief that certain objects could cause separation, possibly related to superstitions or folklore.
    • 西秦乞伏熾磐都長安 - Refers to the Western Qin ruler Qifu Chikhan establishing his capital in Chang'an.

    During the early years of the Long'an era, dogs in the Wu Prefecture area were often barking at night, gathering on the Gao Bridge. Despite the limited number of dogs, their barking was numerous. Someone observed the situation at night and saw a dog with two or three heads, all facing forward and barking chaotically. Shortly after, there was a rebellion led by Sun En.

    During the reign of Emperor Xie of Jin, there was a gathering of important people where a type of food or grain was hidden. Among the arms, a hand-like object was found, with a long bone, large fingers, and coarse black fur. Everyone present was startled. Soon after, the person was killed by Marshal Huán. An old belief held that certain objects could cause separation, and this belief was deeply verified.

    In the twelfth year of Emperor Fu Jian's reign, a large turtle measuring three feet and six inches was found in a well in Gaoling County. The turtle had ancient characters representing the Eight Trigrams on its back. Emperor Fu Jian ordered a stone pool to be built to keep the turtle and fed it millet. After the turtle died, its bones were buried in the imperial ancestral temple. That night, the temple steward named Gao Lü dreamed that the turtle spoke to him, saying it was originally destined to return to the south but met an unfortunate fate in the Qin court. Someone in the dream told Gao Lü that the turtle, having lived for 3600 years, predicted that its death would bring disaster and the downfall of the state. Shortly after, Xie Xuan defeated the Qin forces at the Battle of Fei River, and the turtle's prediction came true.

    Murong Ren went hunting and encountered an old man who said, "This is not a place for hunting, Your Highness should return." The next morning, Murong Ren went hunting again and encountered a white rabbit. He chased the rabbit, fell from his horse, and died.

    In the Western Qin, Qifu Chikhan established his capital in Chang'an. In front of the main gate, there was a well where people often stayed overnight at the water pavilion to draw water. One night, they heard a tapping sound and were startled. They lit a lantern and saw red water in a jar with a red fish, about three inches long and with a one-inch glow. At that time, the Eastern Qiang and Western barbarians were fighting each other, and the country soon fell. [31]

    佛佛虏凶虐暴恶，常自言国名佛佛，则是佛中之佛。寻被震死。既葬，而复就冢中霹雳其柩，引身出外，题背四字，表其凶逆而然也。国少时为涉去所袭。元嘉十九年，京口霹雳杀人，亦自题背。
    　　凉州张字安逊，夜寝，忽见屋梁间有人像无头，久而乃灭。甚恶之，寻为左右所害。
    　　卢龙将寇乱京师，谣言曰“十丈瓦屋，芦作柱，薤作栏”。未几而败。
    　　文帝元嘉末，长广人病差，便能食而不得卧，一饭辄觉身长。如此数日，头遂出屋。假究为刺史，度之为三丈，复还渐缩如旧，经日而亡。俄而文帝为元凶所害。
    　　陈仲弓从诸子侄造荀季和父子，于时德星聚，太史奏五百里内有贤人聚。
    　　陶侃左手有文直达中指，上横节便止。有相者师圭谓侃曰：“君左手中指有竖理，若彻于上，位在无极。”侃以针挑令彻，血流弹壁，乃作公字。又取纸裹，公迹愈明。 [32]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [32]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 佛佛虏凶虐暴恶 - Compound term describing the tyrannical and cruel nature of the 'Fofu' people.
    • 常自言国名佛佛，则是佛中之佛 - Self-proclaimed statement indicating their belief in being the 'Buddha of Buddhas.'
    • 寻被震死，既葬，而复就冢中霹雳其柩，引身出外，题背四字，表其凶逆而然也 - Sequence of events where the body is struck by lightning after burial and then re-emerges with symbolic markings.
    • 国少时为涉去所袭 - Indicates that the country was attacked by 'Shequ' in its early days.
    • 元嘉十九年，京口霹雳杀人，亦自题背 - Historical event where lightning killed someone in Jingkou, with similar markings on the body.
    • 凉州张字安逊，夜寝，忽见屋梁间有人像无头，久而乃灭 - Vision or dream of a headless figure on the roof beam, which eventually disappears.
    • 甚恶之，寻为左右所害 - He strongly disliked it and soon was killed by his attendants.
    • 卢龙将寇乱京师，谣言曰“十丈瓦屋，芦作柱，薤作栏” - Prophecy or rumor about the chaos caused by Lu Long's rebellion.
    • 未几而败 - The rebellion was soon defeated.
    • 文帝元嘉末，长广人病差，便能食而不得卧，一饭辄觉身长，如此数日，头遂出屋 - Person who could eat but could not lie down after recovering from an illness, and after each meal, he felt taller, eventually his head protruded through the roof.
    • 假究为刺史，度之为三丈，复还渐缩如旧，经日而亡 - Temporary governor measured him at three zhang (about 9 meters), and he gradually shrank back to normal before dying.
    • 俄而文帝为元凶所害 - Soon after, Emperor Wen was killed by Yuanxiong.
    • 陈仲弓从诸子侄造荀季和父子 - Chen Zhonggong visited the father and son of Xun Jihuo with his nephews.
    • 于时德星聚，太史奏五百里内有贤人聚 - At that time, the virtuous stars gathered, and the historian reported that there were virtuous people within 500 li.
    • 陶侃左手有文直达中指，上横节便止 - Pattern on Tao Kan's left hand.
    • 有相者师圭谓侃曰：“君左手中指有竖理，若彻于上，位在无极。” - Diviner named Shi Gui told Tao Kan: 'Your left middle finger has a vertical line; if it extends upward, your position will be limitless.'
    • 侃以针挑令彻，血流弹壁，乃作公字。 - Tao Kan used a needle to prick it, and blood flowed onto the wall, forming the character 'gong' (公).
    • 又取纸裹，公迹愈明 - He then wrapped it in paper, and the character 'gong' became even clearer.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 佛佛虏 - Refers to a historical group or people known as 'Fofu,' possibly a tribal or ethnic group.
    • 元嘉 - Refers to the reign title of Emperor Wen of Liu Song (宋文帝).
    • 京口 - Refers to a city in present-day Jiangsu Province, historically significant during the Southern Dynasties period.
    • 凉州 - Refers to a region in present-day Gansu Province, historically an important area during the Han Dynasty and later periods.
    • 长广 - Refers to a place in present-day Shandong Province, historically significant during the Eastern Jin and Southern Dynasties periods.
    • 卢龙 - Refers to a historical figure or place associated with rebellion or military action.
    • 文帝 - Refers to Emperor Wen of Liu Song (宋文帝).
    • 陈仲弓 - Refers to Chen Zhonggong, a historical figure.
    • 荀季和 - Refers to Xun Jihuo, a historical figure.
    • 陶侃 - Refers to Tao Kan, a historical figure known for his military and political achievements.
    • 德星 - Refers to stars associated with virtue or moral excellence in Chinese astrology.
    • 太史 - Refers to the official historian or astrologer in ancient China.
    • 无极 - Refers to a concept of boundlessness or infinity in Chinese philosophy.

    The Fofu people were cruel and violent, often claiming that their country was named Fofu, thus calling themselves the "Buddha of Buddhas." They were soon struck dead by lightning. After they were buried, their coffin was struck by lightning again in the tomb, and their body emerged from the grave, with four characters written on its back, symbolizing their wickedness. In its early days, the country was attacked by Shequ. In the nineteenth year of the Yuanjia era, a man in Jingkou was struck by lightning and killed, with similar markings on his body.

    Zhang Anxun from Liangzhou, while sleeping one night, suddenly saw a headless figure on the roof beam, which eventually disappeared. He strongly disliked this vision and soon was killed by his attendants.

    Lu Long led a rebellion that caused chaos in the capital, and there was a prophecy that said, "A ten-zhang tile house, with reeds as pillars and garlic as railings." The rebellion was soon defeated.

    In the final years of the Yuanjia era, a man from Changguang recovered from an illness but could eat but not lie down. After each meal, he felt taller, and after several days, his head protruded through the roof. A temporary governor measured him at three zhang (about 9 meters), and he gradually shrank back to normal before dying. Soon after, Emperor Wen was killed by Yuanxiong.

    Chen Zhonggong visited the father and son of Xun Jihuo with his nephews. At that time, the virtuous stars gathered, and the historian reported that there were virtuous people within 500 li.

    Tao Kan had a pattern on his left hand that extended directly to his middle finger, stopping at a horizontal line. A diviner named Shi Gui told Tao Kan: "Your left middle finger has a vertical line; if it extends upward, your position will be limitless." Tao Kan used a needle to prick it, and blood flowed onto the wall, forming the character 'gong' (公). He then wrapped it in paper, and the character 'gong' became even clearer. [32]

    桓玄生而有光照室，善占者云：“此儿生有奇曜，宜目为天人。”宣武嫌其三文，复言为神灵宝，犹复用三。既难重前，却减神一字，名曰灵宝。
    　　任城魏肇之初生，有雀飞入其手，占者以为封爵之祥。
    　　东莞刘穆之字道和，小字道人，世居京口。隆安中，凤凰集其庭，相人韦薮谓之曰：“子必协赞大猷。”
    　　徐羡之年少时，尝有一人来谓曰：“我是汝祖。”羡之拜，此人曰：“汝有贵相而有大厄，宜以钱二十八文埋宅四角，可以免灾。过此，位极人臣。”后羡之随亲之县，住在县内，尝暂出而贼自后破县，县内人无免者，鸡犬亦尽，惟羡之在外获全。
    　　晋陵韦朗家在延陵，元嘉初，忽见庭前井中有人出，齐长尺余，被带组甲，麾伍相应，相随出门。良久乃尽。朗兄薮颇善占筮，尝云：“吾子当至刺史。”后朗历刺青、广二州。 [33]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [33]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 生而有光照室 - Literal expression meaning 'at birth there was light illuminating the room,' used to describe an auspicious sign at birth.
    • 善占者云 - Means 'a skilled diviner said,' introducing a divinatory interpretation.
    • 宜目为天人 - Means 'should be regarded as a heavenly being,' suggesting an extraordinary destiny.
    • 宣武嫌其三文 - Means 'Xuanwu disliked the three characters,' referring to the emperor's disapproval of the name's complexity.
    • 复言为神灵宝 - Means 'then said it should be the divine treasure,' another suggested name.
    • 既难重前，却减神一字，名曰灵宝 - Means 'since it was difficult to repeat the previous one, he reduced it by one character, naming it 'Lingbao,' explaining the final naming decision.
    • 任城魏肇之初生 - Means 'when Weizhao of Rencheng was born,' introducing a new subject.
    • 有雀飞入其手 - Means 'a sparrow flew into his hand,' describing an auspicious sign.
    • 占者以为封爵之祥 - Means 'the diviner thought it was an omen of a noble title,' interpreting the sign.
    • 尝有一人来谓曰 - Means 'once a person came and said,' introducing a supernatural encounter.
    • 汝有贵相而有大厄 - Means 'you have a noble appearance but also a great calamity,' describing a mixed fortune.
    • 宜以钱二十八文埋宅四角 - Means 'should bury twenty-eight coins at the four corners of the house,' giving a remedy.
    • 过此，位极人臣 - Means 'after this, you will reach the highest rank among ministers,' predicting a future high position.
    • 后羡之随亲之县，住在县内 - Means 'later Xianzhi followed his parents to the county and lived inside it,' setting up the context for the next event.
    • 尝暂出而贼自后破县 - Means 'once he briefly left and the bandits broke the county behind him,' describing a dangerous situation.
    • 县内人无免者，鸡犬亦尽 - Means 'no one in the county escaped, not even the chickens and dogs,' emphasizing the severity of the attack.
    • 惟羡之在外获全 - Means 'only Xianzhi outside was safe,' highlighting his survival.
    • 晋陵韦朗家在延陵 - Means 'Wei Lang of Jinling lived in Yanling,' introducing another subject.
    • 元嘉初，忽见庭前井中有人出 - Means 'in the early years of Yuangjia, he suddenly saw someone emerge from the well in front of his courtyard,' describing an unusual event.
    • 齐长尺余，被带组甲，麾伍相应，相随出门 - Means 'they were about a foot long, wearing belts and armor, leading their troops in response, and followed out the door,' describing the appearance and behavior of the figures.
    • 良久乃尽 - Means 'after a long time, they all disappeared,' concluding the event.
    • 吾子当至刺史 - Means 'my son will become a governor,' predicting a future high position.
    • 后朗历刺青、广二州 - Means 'later Lang served as governor of Qing and Guang provinces,' confirming the prediction.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 桓玄 - A historical figure, the son of the famous general Huan Wen, who later became a usurper.
    • 宣武 - Refers to Emperor An of Jin, who ruled during the period described.
    • 任城魏肇之 - A historical figure, a nobleman of the Jin dynasty.
    • 徐羡之 - A historical figure, a prominent official of the Jin dynasty.
    • 晋陵 - Refers to Jinling, an ancient name for Nanjing, the capital of the Jin dynasty.
    • 延陵 - Refers to Yanling, a place name in the region.
    • 元嘉 - Refers to the era name of Emperor Wen of Liu Song, the first emperor of the Southern Song dynasty.
    • 刺史 - Refers to a high-ranking official in charge of a province.
    • 青、广二州 - Refers to the provinces of Qing and Guang, which were important administrative regions during the period.

    Proper Nouns

    • 桓玄 - The historical figure.
    • 宣武 - The emperor.
    • 任城魏肇之 - The nobleman.
    • 徐羡之 - The official.
    • 晋陵 - The place name.
    • 延陵 - The place name.
    • 元嘉 - The era name.
    • 刺史 - The official title.
    • 青、广二州 - The province names.

    Huan Xuan was born with a light illuminating the room, and a skilled diviner said, "This child was born with a unique brilliance and should be regarded as a heavenly being." Emperor An disliked the three characters in his name, so he suggested calling him "Shenlingbao." However, it was difficult to repeat the previous name, so he reduced it by one character, naming him "Lingbao."

    When Weizhao of Rencheng was born, a sparrow flew into his hand, and the diviner thought it was an omen of a noble title.

    Dongguan Liu Mu, also known as Daohe and by the nickname Dao Ren, lived in Jingkou. During the Long'an period, a phoenix gathered in his courtyard, and the diviner Wei Sou said to him, "You will surely assist in great plans."

    When Xu Xianzhi was young, once a person came and said, "I am your ancestor." Xianzhi bowed, and the person said, "You have a noble appearance but also a great calamity. You should bury twenty-eight coins at the four corners of your house to avoid disaster. After this, you will reach the highest rank among ministers." Later, Xianzhi followed his parents to the county and lived inside it. Once he briefly left, and the bandits broke the county behind him. No one in the county escaped, not even the chickens and dogs. Only Xianzhi outside was safe.

    Wei Lang of Jinling lived in Yanling. In the early years of Yuangjia, he suddenly saw someone emerge from the well in front of his courtyard. They were about a foot long, wearing belts and armor, leading their troops in response, and followed out the door. After a long time, they all disappeared. Wei Sou, Wei Lang's elder brother, was skilled in divination and said, "My son will become a governor." Later, Lang served as governor of Qing and Guang provinces. [33]

    晋贾谥字长渊，充子也。元康九年六月，夜暴雷震谥斋屋，柱陷入地，压毁床帐。飘风吹其朝服上天数百丈，久之乃坠于中丞台。又蛇出其被中。谥甚恐，明年伏诛。
    　　晋孝武太元年，刘波字道则，移居京口。昼寝，闻屏风外悒咤声，开屏风，见一狗蹲地而语，语毕自去。波，隗孙也，后为前将军，败，见杀。
    　　晋太始中，豫州刺史彭城刘德愿镇寿阳。住内屋，闭户未合，辄有人头进门扉，窥看户内，是丈夫露髻团面。内人惊告，把火搜觅，了不见人。刘明年竟被诛。
    　　河南褚裒字季野，将北伐，军士忽同时唱言“可各持两。”复相谓曰“一人焉用两为！”及败北，抛戈弃甲，两手各持一，蒙首而奔。
    　　晋安帝义熙三年，殷仲文为东阳太守。尝照镜，不见其面，俄而难及。 [34]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [34]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 晉賈謐字長淵 - A proper name and courtesy name of a person from the Jin dynasty.
    • 充子也 - Indicates that 賈謐 is the son of 賈充.
    • 夜暴雷震 - Describes a sudden and violent thunderstorm at night.
    • 柱陷入地 - Means that a pillar sank into the ground.
    • 壓毀床帳 - Describes the destruction of beds and curtains.
    • 飄風吹其朝服上天 - Describes a strong wind that blew his official robes into the sky.
    • 久之乃墜於中丞台 - Indicates that after a long time, the robes fell onto the Zhongcheng Platform.
    • 蛇出其被中 - Describes a snake emerging from his bedding.
    • 甚恐 - Means he was very afraid.
    • 明年伏誅 - Indicates that he was executed the following year.
    • 晉孝武太元年 - Refers to the year of the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 劉波字道則 - A proper name and courtesy name of a person.
    • 移居京口 - Means to move to Jingkou (modern-day Zhenjiang).
    • 昼寝 - Means to take a nap during the day.
    • 聞屏風外悒咤聲 - Describes hearing a sighing or grumbling sound outside the screen.
    • 開屏風 - Means to open the screen.
    • 見一狗蹲地而語 - Describes seeing a dog sitting on the ground and speaking.
    • 語畢自去 - Means the dog spoke and then left on its own.
    • 隗孫也 - Indicates that 刘波 is a descendant of 刘隗.
    • 後為前將軍，敗，見殺 - Describes his later career, defeat, and execution.
    • 晉太始中 - Refers to the period of Emperor Taiwu of Jin.
    • 豫州刺史彭城劉德願鎮壽陽 - A proper name and title of a person, indicating he was the governor of Yuzhou and stationed in Shouyang.
    • 住內屋，閉戶未合 - Describes him living in an inner room with the door not fully closed.
    • 見有人頭进门扉 - Describes seeing a head entering through the door.
    • 是丈夫露髻團面 - Describes the head as that of a man with a bare head and round face.
    • 內人驚告 - Means the women inside were alarmed and reported it.
    • 把火搜覓 - Describes searching with a torch.
    • 了不見人 - Means they saw no one.
    • 劉明年竟被誅 - Indicates that he was executed the following year.
    • 河南褚裒字季野 - A proper name and courtesy name of a person.
    • 將北伐 - Means he was preparing to launch a northern campaign.
    • 軍士忽同時唱言 - Describes soldiers suddenly shouting in unison.
    • 可各持兩 - Means 'each should hold two spears.'
    • 復相謂曰 - Means they then said to each other.
    • 一人焉用兩為 - Means 'why would one person need two spears?'
    • 及敗北 - Describes the defeat.
    • 抛戈棄甲 - Means throwing away weapons and armor.
    • 蒙首而奔 - Describes running with their heads covered.
    • 晉安帝義熙三年 - Refers to the year of the reign of Emperor An of Jin.
    • 殷仲文為東陽太守 - A proper name and title of a person.
    • 嘗照鏡 - Means he once looked into a mirror.
    • 不見其面 - Means he could not see his face.
    • 俄而難及 - Describes a sudden difficulty or trouble.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晉賈謐 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 賈充 - A prominent politician and military leader of the Jin dynasty.
    • 晉孝武太元年 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 劉波 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 劉隗 - A historical figure, an ancestor of 刘波.
    • 晉太始中 - A specific period during the reign of Emperor Taiwu of Jin.
    • 豫州刺史 - A high-ranking official position in the Jin dynasty.
    • 彭城 - A place name, modern-day Xuzhou.
    • 壽陽 - A place name, modern-day Shouyang.
    • 河南褚裒 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 晉安帝義熙三年 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor An of Jin.
    • 殷仲文 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 東陽 - A place name, modern-day Shangyu.

    Proper Nouns

    • 晉賈謐 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 賈充 - A prominent politician and military leader of the Jin dynasty.
    • 晉孝武太元年 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin.
    • 劉波 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 劉隗 - A historical figure, an ancestor of 刘波.
    • 晉太始中 - A specific period during the reign of Emperor Taiwu of Jin.
    • 豫州刺史 - A high-ranking official position in the Jin dynasty.
    • 彭城 - A place name, modern-day Xuzhou.
    • 壽陽 - A place name, modern-day Shouyang.
    • 河南褚裒 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 晉安帝義熙三年 - A specific year during the reign of Emperor An of Jin.
    • 殷仲文 - A historical figure from the Jin dynasty.
    • 東陽 - A place name, modern-day Shangyu.

    Jin Dynasty: Jia Mi, courtesy name Changyuan, was the son of Jia Chong. In the ninth year of the Yuankang era (298 AD), in June, a violent thunderstorm struck his study at night, causing a pillar to sink into the ground and collapse the beds and curtains. A strong wind blew his official robes into the sky for hundreds of feet, and after a long time, they fell onto the Zhongcheng Platform. A snake also emerged from his bedding. Jia Mi was very afraid, and the following year he was executed.

    In the first year of the Taoyuan era of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (371 AD), Liu Bo, courtesy name Daoze, moved to Jingkou. During the day, he took a nap and heard a sighing or grumbling sound outside the screen. When he opened the screen, he saw a dog sitting on the ground and speaking. After speaking, the dog left on its own. Liu Bo was a descendant of Liu Wei, and later he became a general, but he was defeated and executed.

    In the early years of the Taishi era of Emperor Taiwu of Jin, Liu Deyuan, governor of Yuzhou and stationed in Shouyang, lived in an inner room with the door not fully closed. Suddenly, a head entered through the door, looking into the room, and it was that of a man with a bare head and round face. The women inside were alarmed and reported it, searching with a torch but finding no one. Liu Deyuan was executed the following year.

    In the early years of the Taishi era, Chu Pao, courtesy name Jiye, from Henan, was preparing to launch a northern campaign. Suddenly, the soldiers shouted in unison, "Each should hold two spears." They then said to each other, "Why would one person need two spears?" When they were defeated and retreated, they threw away their weapons and armor, holding one spear in each hand, and ran with their heads covered.

    In the third year of the Yixi era of Emperor An of Jin (407 AD), Yin Zhongwen served as the governor of Dongyang. Once, when he looked into a mirror, he could not see his face, and soon afterward, he encountered difficulties. [34]

    元帝永昌元年，丹阳甘卓将袭王敦，既而中止。及还家，多变怪，自照镜不见其头，乃视庭树，而头在树上，心甚恶之。先时，历阳陈训私谓所亲曰：“甘侯头低而视仰，相法名为盼刀。又目有赤脉，自外而入。不出十年，必以兵死。不领兵则可以免。”至是果为敦所袭。
    　　东晋谢安字安石，于后府接宾。妇刘氏见狗衔谢头来，久之乃失所在。妇具说之，谢容色无易，是月而薨。
    　　晋阮明泊舟西浦，见一青衣女子弯弓射之，女即轩云而去。明寻被害。
    　　义熙中，王愉字茂和，在庭中行，帽忽自落，仍乘空如人所著。及愉母丧月朝上祭酒器在几上，须臾下地，复还登床。寻而第三儿缓怀贰伏诛。
    　　晋隆安中，高惠清为太傅主簿。忽一日有群鼠更相衔尾，自屋梁相连至地。清寻得症疾，数日而亡。
    　　晋桓振在淮南，夜间人登床声，振听之，隐然有声。求火看之，见大聚血。俄为义师所灭桓振，玄之从父弟也。 [35]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [35]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 盼刀 - Idiom or fortune-telling term, possibly indicating a bad omen or impending doom.
    • 赤脉 - Red streaks, often seen as an ominous sign in facial features.
    • 兵死 - Die by military means.
    • 不领兵则可以免 - If not leading troops, one can avoid the danger.
    • 轩云而去 - Fly away like a cloud.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 东晋 - The Eastern Jin dynasty, a period in Chinese history from 317 to 420 CE.
    • 谢安 - A famous statesman and military leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 刘氏 - The wife of Xie An.
    • 狗衔谢头来 - A supernatural event where a dog brings Xie An's head.
    • 晋阮明 - A person named Ruan Ming during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 晋桓振 - A person named Huan Zhen during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 玄之从父弟 - Brother of Huan Xuan, a powerful warlord during the Eastern Jin dynasty.

    Proper Nouns

    • 元帝 - Emperor Yuan of Eastern Jin.
    • 永昌元年 - 345 CE.
    • 丹阳 - A region in southern China.
    • 甘卓 - A person from Dan Yang.
    • 王敦 - A prominent figure during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 历阳 - A region in Anhui province.
    • 陈训 - A person from Liyang.
    • 甘侯 - A respectful title for Gan Zhuo.
    • 西浦 - A western riverbank.
    • 阮明 - A person named Ruan Ming.
    • 青衣女子 - A woman dressed in blue.
    • 义熙中 - 403-418 CE.
    • 王愉 - A person named Wang Yu.
    • 茂和 - A respectful title for Wang Yu.
    • 桓振 - A person named Huan Zhen.
    • 淮南 - The region of Huainan.
    • 义师 - Righteous army.

    In the first year of Yongchang during the reign of Emperor Yuan, Gan Zhuo from Danyang was planning to attack Wang Dun, but the plan was subsequently aborted. Upon returning home, he experienced many strange occurrences. When he looked into a mirror, he could not see his head; instead, he looked at the trees in the courtyard and saw his head on one of them. He was greatly disturbed by this. Previously, Chen Xun from Liyang had privately told his close associates, "Gan Hou's head is low while his gaze is upward, which in physiognomy is called 'pan dao.' Moreover, there are red streaks in his eyes, entering from the outside. Within ten years, he will surely die by military means. If he does not lead troops, he can avoid this." Indeed, Gan Zhuo was later attacked by Wang Dun.

    During the Eastern Jin dynasty, Xie An, also known as Anshi, received guests at his official residence. His wife, Liu, saw a dog carrying Xie An's head. After a while, the dog disappeared. Liu recounted this to Xie An, who remained calm. That month, Xie An died.

    In the Eastern Jin dynasty, Ruan Ming moored his boat at the western bank. He saw a woman dressed in blue stringing her bow and shooting at him. The woman then flew away like a cloud. Ruan Ming was soon killed.

    During the reign of Emperor An in the fourth year of Yixi, Wang Yu, also known as Muhuo, was walking in the courtyard when his hat suddenly fell off. It then flew up into the air as if someone had put it there. When Wang Yu's mother's memorial service began, the wine vessel on the table suddenly fell to the ground and then returned to the table. Soon after, his third son, Huan, was executed for treason.

    During the reign of Emperor An in the fourth year of Long'an, Gao Huiqing served as the chief clerk of the Grand Tutor. One day, a group of mice began to link tails together, forming a chain that stretched from the eaves to the ground. Gao Huiqing soon fell ill and died within a few days.

    In the region of Huainan, Huan Zhen heard a sound of someone climbing onto his bed at night. When he listened closely, he heard a faint sound. Upon lighting a fire to investigate, he saw a large pool of blood. Soon after, Huan Zhen was defeated by the righteous army. Huan Zhen was the brother of Huan Xuan. [35]

    义熙中，刘毅镇江州，为卢循所败，忄扁忄逾剧。及徙荆州，益复怏怏，尝伸纸作书，约部将王亮连兵作逆。忽风，展纸不得书，毅仰天大诟，风遂吹纸入空，须臾碎裂，如飞雪纷下。未几，高祖南讨，毅败擒斩。
    　　永初中，北地传亮为护军，兄子珍住府西斋。夜忽见北窗外树下有一物面广三尺，眼横竖，状若方相。珍遑遽以被自蒙，久乃自灭。后亮被诛。
    　　元嘉中，高平檀道济镇浔阳，十二年入朝，与家分别，顾瞻城阙，欷逾深，识者是知道济之不南旋也。故时人为其歌曰：“生人作死别，荼毒当奈何。”济将发舟，所养孔雀来衔其衣，驱去复来，如此数焉。”以十三年三月入伏诛。道济未下，少时有人施罟于柴桑江，收之，得大船，孔凿若新。使匠作舴艋，勿加断斧。工人误截两头，檀以为不祥。杀三巧手，欲以塞侃言。匠违约加斫，凶兆先构矣。 [36]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [36]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 義熙中 - Refers to the period of the reign of Emperor Xiaowu of Liu Song (405–424 AD).
    • 盧循所敗 - Indicates defeat by Lu Xun, a rebel leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 忄扁忄逾劇 - Describes extreme anxiety or agitation.
    • 徙荊州 - Refers to being transferred to the position of governor of Jingzhou.
    • 益復怏怏 - Expresses increasing discontent or dissatisfaction.
    • 伸紙作書 - Means to write a letter or document.
    • 約部將王亮連兵作逆 - Refers to plotting with a subordinate general named Wang Liang to rebel.
    • 忽風，展紙不得書 - Describes a sudden wind that prevents writing.
    • 仰天大诟 - Means to curse or swear loudly to the sky.
    • 展紙不得書 - Indicates that the wind blew the paper away, preventing further writing.
    • 毅仰天大诟，風遂吹紙入空，須臾碎裂，如飛雪纷下 - Describes the dramatic event where the wind blows the paper into the air and tears it apart like falling snow.
    • 未幾，高祖南討，毅敗擒斬 - Refers to the subsequent events where Liu Song's forces, led by Emperor Xiaowu, defeated and captured Liu Yi.
    • 永初中 - Refers to the early years of the Liu Song dynasty (420–479 AD).
    • 北地傳亮為護軍 - Indicates that Wang Liang was appointed as a military commander in the north.
    • 兄子珍住府西齋 - Refers to Wang Liang's nephew, who lived in the west pavilion of the government office.
    • 夜忽見北窗外樹下有一物面廣三尺，眼橫竖，狀若方相 - Describes a supernatural vision of a face three feet wide with horizontal and vertical eyes, resembling a demon.
    • 珍遑遽以被自蒙，久乃自滅 - Means that Wang Liang's nephew hastily covered himself with a blanket, and the vision eventually disappeared.
    • 后亮被诛 - Refers to the execution of Wang Liang.
    • 元嘉中 - Refers to the middle period of the Liu Song dynasty (424–453 AD).
    • 高平檀道濟鎮浔陽 - Indicates that Tan Daoji, a military general from Gaoping, was stationed in Xunyang.
    • 十二年入朝，與家分別，顧瞻城闕，欷逾深 - Describes Tan Daoji's return to the capital in the twelfth year of his service, where he bid farewell to his family and looked back at the city gates with deep emotion.
    • 識者是知道濟之不南旋也 - Means that wise people recognized that Tan Daoji would not return south.
    • 故時人為其歌曰：“生人作死別，荼毒當奈何” - Refers to a folk song that expressed the sorrow of a living person saying goodbye to a loved one, as if they were already dead.
    • 濟將發舟，所養孔雀來銜其衣，驅去復來，如此數焉 - Describes the supernatural event where Tan Daoji's pet peacock repeatedly bit his clothes as he prepared to leave.
    • 以十三年三月入伏誅 - Refers to the execution of Tan Daoji in the thirteenth year of his service.
    • 道濟未下，少時有人施罟於柴桑江，收之，得大船，孔鑿若新 - Describes the discovery of a newly made ship in Chaisang River.
    • 使匠作舴艋，勿加斷斧 - Means that the craftsmen were instructed not to use a saw to cut the wood.
    • 工人誤截兩頭，檀以為不祥 - Refers to the mistake made by the workers, which Tan Daoji believed was an omen of bad luck.
    • 殺三巧手，欲以塞侃言 - Means that Tan Daoji killed three skilled workers to silence their warnings.
    • 匠違約加斫，凶兆先構矣 - Refers to the craftsmen disobeying the order and using a saw, which was seen as a bad omen.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 荊州 - A region in southern China, historically significant for its strategic importance.
    • 永初中 - The early years of the Liu Song dynasty (420–479 AD).
    • 北地 - The northern regions of China.
    • 護軍 - A high-ranking military position.
    • 元嘉中 - The middle period of the Liu Song dynasty (424–453 AD).
    • 高平 - A place name, likely referring to a region in modern-day Shandong Province.
    • 浔陽 - A city in modern-day Jiangxi Province.
    • 元嘉十三年 - The thirteenth year of the reign of Emperor Wen of Liu Song (435 AD).

    Proper Nouns

    • 劉毅 - Liu Yi, a military general during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 盧循 - Lu Xun, a rebel leader during the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 王亮 - Wang Liang, a subordinate general of Liu Yi.
    • 高祖 - Emperor Xiaowu of Liu Song (r. 424–464 AD).
    • 元嘉 - The era name of Emperor Wen of Liu Song (r. 424–453 AD).
    • 檀道濟 - Tan Daoji, a military general during the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 柴桑 - A city in modern-day Jiangxi Province.

    义熙年间，刘毅镇守江州，被卢循击败，心情极度焦虑。后来他调任荆州，更加郁郁不乐，曾铺开纸写信，约部将王亮联合军队造反。忽然一阵风起，纸被吹走无法继续书写，刘毅仰天大骂，风将纸吹入空中，不久碎裂如飞雪纷飞。不久后，高祖南征，刘毅战败被擒斩。

    永初年间，北地传王亮为护军，他的侄子王珍住在府西斋。一天夜里，王珍忽然看到北窗外树下有一个宽三尺的脸庞，眼睛横竖排列，形状像方相。王珍急忙用被子蒙住自己，过了很久才消失。后来王亮被诛杀。

    元嘉年间，高平的檀道济镇守浔阳，十二年入朝，与家人分别时，望着城门叹息不已，深知者认为檀道济不会返回南方。因此当时人为他唱道：“生人作死别，荼毒当奈何。”檀道济准备出发时，他养的孔雀来咬他的衣服，赶走又回来，如此反复几次。到了十三年三月，檀道济被伏诛。檀道济未下船前，有人在柴桑江捕鱼，捞上来一艘新凿的大船。檀道济让工匠制作小船，不要用锯子。工匠误截了两端，檀道济认为这是不祥之兆，杀了三个巧匠，想以此堵住他们的嘴。工匠违背命令使用锯子，凶兆已经显现。 [36]

    檀道济居清溪，第二儿夜忽见人来缚己，欲呼不得，至晓乃解，犹见绳痕。在此宅先是吴将步阐所居，谚云：扬州青，是鬼营。清溪青，扬是也。自步及檀，皆被诛。
    　　东海徐羡之字宗文，尝行经山中，见黑龙长丈余，头有角，前两足皆具，无后足，曳尾而行。后文帝立羡之，竟以凶终。
    　　太元中王公妇女必缓鬓倾髻以为盛饰。用既多，不可恒戴，乃先于木及笼上装之，名曰“假髻”，或名“假头”。至于贫家不能自办，自号“无头”，就人借头。
    　　文帝元嘉四年，太原王徽之字伯猷为交州刺史。在道，有客命索酒炙，言未讫而炙至。徽之取自割，终不食投地，大怒。少顷，顾视向炙已变为徽之头矣，乃大惊愕，反属目睹，其首在空中挥霍而没，至州便殒。 [37]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [37]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.
    • 在此宅先是吳將步闡所居 - Indicates that the house was previously occupied by Wu general Bu Chan.
    • 揚州青，是鬼營 - Proverb suggesting that 'Yangzhou is green, it is a ghost camp.'
    • 清溪青，揚是也 - Indicates that 'Qingxi is green, Yangzhou is also like this.'
    • 自步及檀，皆被誅 - Indicates that both Bu Chan and Tan Daoji were executed.
    • 太元中王公婦女必緩鬢傾髻以為盛飾 - Describes the fashion trend among noble women during the Taiyuan period, where they wore their hair in a loose and slanted style.
    • 用既多，不可恒戴 - Indicates that since there was a lot of hair, it could not be worn constantly.
    • 乃先於木及籠上裝之，名曰‘假髻’，或名‘假頭’ - Describes the practice of using wooden or wicker frames to support the hair, called 'false hair' or 'false head.'
    • 至於貧家不能自辦，自號‘無頭’，就人借頭 - Describes poor families who could not afford to make their own false hair, so they would call themselves 'headless' and borrow hair from others.
    • 少頃，顧視向炙已變為徽之頭矣 - Describes how after a short while, when he looked back, the roasted meat had turned into his own head.
    • 乃大驚愕，反屬目睹，其首在空中揮霍而沒，至州便殞 - Describes how he was greatly shocked, looking back and seeing his head flying through the air and disappearing, and he died upon reaching the state.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 檀道濟 - A famous general of the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 清溪 - A place name, possibly referring to a river or a region.
    • 吳將步闡 - A general of the Wu state during the Three Kingdoms period.
    • 揚州 - A historical region in China.
    • 東海徐羡之 - A historical figure from the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 太元 - The era name of Emperor Xiaowu of Eastern Jin.
    • 王徽之 - A historical figure from the Eastern Jin dynasty.
    • 交州 - A historical region in southern China.
    • 文帝元嘉四年 - Refers to the fourth year of the Yuangjia era of Emperor Wen of Liu Song.
    • 太原 - A historical region in China.
    • 伯猷 - A courtesy name of Wang Hui.
    • 元嘉 - The era name of Emperor Wen of Liu Song.

    Proper Nouns

    • 檀道濟 - A historical figure.
    • 清溪 - A place name.
    • 吳將步闡 - A historical figure.
    • 揚州 - A historical region.
    • 東海徐羡之 - A historical figure.
    • 太元 - An era name.
    • 王徽之 - A historical figure.
    • 交州 - A historical region.
    • 文帝元嘉四年 - A specific year.
    • 太原 - A historical region.
    • 伯猷 - A courtesy name.
    • 元嘉 - An era name.

    Tan Daoji lived in Qingxi, and one night his second son suddenly saw someone come to tie him up. He wanted to call for help but could not, and it was not until dawn that the ropes were released, leaving visible marks. The house had previously been occupied by Wu general Bu Chan, and there is a saying: "Yangzhou is green, it is a ghost camp." Qingxi is also like this. From Bu Chan to Tan Daoji, both were executed.

    Xu Xianzhi from Donghai, also known as Zongwen, once traveled through the mountains and saw a black dragon about ten feet long. It had horns on its head, front legs, but no back legs, dragging its tail. After Emperor Wen established Xu Xianzhi, he ultimately met a tragic end.

    During the Taiyuan period, noble women would wear their hair in a loose and slanted style as a fashionable adornment. Since there was a lot of hair, it could not be worn constantly, so they would first attach it to wooden or wicker frames, which were called "false hair" or "false head." Poor families who could not afford to make their own false hair would call themselves "headless" and borrow hair from others.

    In the fourth year of the Yuangjia era of Emperor Wen, Wang Hui from Taiyuan, also known as Boyou, served as the governor of Jiaozhou. While traveling, a guest asked for wine and roasted meat. Before the guest finished speaking, the roasted meat arrived. Wang Hui cut the meat himself but did not eat it, throwing it on the ground in great anger. After a short while, when he looked back, the roasted meat had turned into his own head. He was greatly shocked, looking back and seeing his head flying through the air and disappearing. He died upon reaching the state. [37]

    彭城刘敬宣字万寿，尝夜与僚佐宴坐，空中有投一只芒履于座，坠敬宣食盘上，长三尺五寸，已经人著，耳鼻间并欲坏，顷之而败。
    　　安国李道豫，元嘉中其家狗卧于当路，豫蹴之，狗曰：“汝即死，何以蹋我！”未几豫死。
    　　卞伯玉作东阳郡，灶正炽火，有鸡遥从口入，良久乃冲突而出，毛羽不憔，鸣啄如故。伯玉寻病殒。
    　　张仲舒为司空，在广陵城北，以元嘉十七年七月中晨夕间辄见门侧有赤气赫然，后空中忽雨绛罗于其庭，广七八分，长五六寸，皆以笺纸系之。纸广长亦与罗等纷纷甚驶。仲舒恶而焚之，犹自数生，府州多相传示。张经宿暴疾而死。
    　　谢灵运以元嘉五年忽见谢晦手提其头，来生别床，血色淋漓，不可忍视。又所服豹皮裘血淹满箧。及为临川郡，饣卞中有大虫，谢遂被诛。 [38]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [38]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 為之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.
    • 嘗夜與僚佐宴坐 - Feasting with subordinates at night.
    • 空中有投一隻芒履於座 - Straw shoe thrown into the seat from the air.
    • 長三尺五寸，已經人著，耳鼻間並欲壞 - Three chi five cun long, already worn, ears and nose almost destroyed.
    • 傾之而敗 - Soon after it decayed.
    • 元嘉中其家狗臥於當路 - Dog lying on the road in the Yuanjia period.
    • 豫蹴之 - Dàoyù kicked it.
    • 汝即死，何以蹋我 - You will die soon, why do you kick me?
    • 未幾豫死 - Soon after, Dàoyù died.
    • 灶正熾火 - Stove burning fiercely.
    • 有雞遙從口入 - Chicken flew in from afar.
    • 良久乃衝突而出 - After a long time, it rushed out.
    • 毛羽不憔，鳴啄如故 - Feathers not damaged, continued to crow and peck as before.
    • 伯玉尋病殞 - Bèiyù soon fell ill and died.
    • 張仲舒為司空，在廣陵城北 - Zhāng Zhōngshū served as the Grand Works Minister in the north of Gǔangling.
    • 以元嘉十七年七月 - In the seventeenth year of the Yuanjia period, in July.
    • 晨夕間輒見門側有赤氣赫然 - In the morning and evening, saw a bright red vapor by the door.
    • 後空中忽雨絳羅於其庭 - From the air, red silk rained down in his courtyard.
    • 廣七八分，長五六寸，皆以箋紙系之 - Seven or eight fen wide, five or six cun long, all tied with paper.
    • 紙廣長亦與羅等紛紛甚駛 - Paper also the same width and length, fell in a flurry.
    • 仲舒惡而焚之 - Zhōngshū burned them out of fear.
    • 猶自數生，府州多相傳示 - They continued to appear, many passed them around to show.
    • 張經宿暴疾而死 - Zhāng died suddenly the next day.
    • 謝靈運以元嘉五年忽見謝晦手提其頭 - Xie Lingyun, in the fifth year of the Yuanjia period, suddenly saw Xie Hui holding his head.
    • 來生別床，血色淋漓，不可忍視 - He came to another bed, blood dripping, unbearable to look at.
    • 又所服豹皮裘血淹滿籠 - Leopard skin coat soaked with blood.
    • 及為臨川郡，饣卞中有大蟲 - When he became the governor of Língchuān, big tiger in his kitchen.
    • 謝遂被誅 - Xie was then executed.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 彭城 - Modern-day Xuzhou, Jiangsu Province.
    • 安國 - Modern-day Anyang, Henan Province.
    • 元嘉 - Reign title of Emperor Wen of Song (424-453 AD).
    • 東陽 - Modern-day Lishui, Zhejiang Province.
    • 廣陵 - Modern-day Yangzhou, Jiangsu Province.
    • 司空 - Equivalent to the Minister of Works.
    • 謝靈運 - Famous poet and official during the Southern Song Dynasty.
    • 謝晦 - High-ranking official during the Southern Song Dynasty, later became a traitor.
    • 臨川 - Modern-day Nanchang, Jiangxi Province.
    • 饣卞中 - Kitchen.
    • 大蟲 - Big tiger, often used in classical literature to refer to dangerous animals.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • 彭城劉敬宣字萬壽，嘗夜與僚佐宴坐，空中有投一隻芒履於座，長三尺五寸，已經人著，耳鼻間並欲壞，傾之而敗。
    • 安國李道豫，元嘉中其家狗臥於當路，豫蹴之，汝即死，何以蹋我，未幾豫死。
    • 卞伯玉作東陽郡，灶正熾火，有雞遙從口入，良久乃衝突而出，毛羽不憔，鳴啄如故，伯玉尋病殞。
    • 張仲舒為司空，在廣陵城北，以元嘉十七年七月，晨夕間輒見門側有赤氣赫然，後空中忽雨絳羅於其庭，廣七八分，長五六寸，皆以箋紙系之，紙廣長亦與羅等紛紛甚駛，仲舒惡而焚之，猶自數生，府州多相傳示，張經宿暴疾而死。
    • 謝靈運以元嘉五年忽見謝晦手提其頭，來生別床，血色淋漓，不可忍視，又所服豹皮裘血淹滿籠，及為臨川郡，饣卞中有大蟲，謝遂被誅。

    Péngchéng Lǐu Jìngxuān, courtesy name Wànshòu, once at night, was feasting with his subordinates when a straw shoe was thrown into the seat from the air and fell onto his food tray. It was three chi five cun long, already worn by someone, with the ears and nose almost destroyed, and soon after it decayed.

    Ānguó Lǐ Dàoyù, during the Yuanjia period, had a dog lying on the road in his family. Dàoyù kicked it, and the dog said, "You will die soon, why do you kick me?" Soon after, Dàoyù died.

    Biàn Bèiyù served as the governor of Dōngyáng. One day, while the stove was burning fiercely, a chicken flew in from afar and after a long time, rushed out. Its feathers were not damaged, and it continued to crow and peck as before. Bèiyù soon fell ill and died.

    Zhāng Zhōngshū served as the Grand Works Minister in the north of Gǔangling. In the seventeenth year of the Yuanjia period, in July, in the morning and evening, he often saw a bright red vapor by the door. Later, from the air, red silk rained down in his courtyard. They were seven or eight fen wide, five or six cun long, all tied with paper. The paper was also the same width and length, and they fell in a flurry. Zhōngshū burned them out of fear, but they still continued to appear, and many in the prefecture and state passed them around to show. Zhāng died suddenly the next day.

    Xie Lingyun, in the fifth year of the Yuanjia period, suddenly saw Xie Hui holding his head and coming to another bed, with blood dripping, unbearable to look at. Also, the leopard skin coat he wore was soaked with blood. When he became the governor of Língchuān, there was a big tiger in his kitchen. Xie was then executed. [38]

    谢晦在荆州，见壁角间有一赤鬼，长可三尺。来至其前，手擎铜盘，满中是血。晦得乃纸盘，须臾而没。
    　　元嘉五年秋夕，豫章胡充有大蜈蚣，长三尺，落充妇与妹前，令婢挟掷。婢才出户，忽睹一姥，衣服臭败，两目无睛。到六年三月，合门时患，死亡相继。
    　　新野庾妻毛氏尝于五月五日曝荐席，忽见其三岁女在席上卧，惊怛便灭。女真形在别床如故。不旬日而夭，世传仲夏忌移床。

    　　●卷五
    　　秦时丹阳县湖侧有梅姑庙。姑生时有道术，能著履行水上。后负道法，婿怒杀之，投尸于水，乃随流波漂至今庙处。钤下巫人当令殡殓，不须坟瘗，即时有方头漆棺在祠堂下，晦朔之日，时见水雾中暖然有著履形，庙左右不得取鱼射猎，辄有，迷径没溺之患。巫云，姑既伤死，所以恶见残杀也。
    　　宫亭湖庙神甚有灵验，商旅经过，若有祷请，则一时能使湖中分风，沿溯皆举帆利涉无虞。 [39]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [39]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 見壁角間有一赤鬼 - Literal description of seeing a red ghost in the corner of the wall.
    • 長可三尺 - Indicates the ghost is about three feet long.
    • 手擎銅盤，滿中是血 - Describes the ghost holding a copper plate full of blood.
    • 須臾而沒 - Means 'disappeared in a moment.'
    • 豫章胡充有大蜈蚣 - Literal description of a large centipede belonging to Hu Chong from Yuzhang.
    • 長三尺 - Indicates the centipede is about three feet long.
    • 落充婦與妹前 - Means 'fell in front of Hu Chong's wife and sister.'
    • 令婢擲 - Means 'ordered the maid to throw it away.'
    • 婢才出戶 - Means 'the maid just stepped out of the door.'
    • 忽睹一姥 - Means 'suddenly saw an old woman.'
    • 衣服臭敗 - Describes the old woman's clothes as smelly and rotten.
    • 兩目無睛 - Means 'with no eyes.'
    • 到六年三月 - Refers to the month of March in the sixth year.
    • 合門時患 - Means 'the whole family suffered from illnesses.'
    • 死亡相繼 - Means 'dying one after another.'
    • 新野庾妻毛氏 - Refers to Mao, the wife of Yu Zhi from Xinye.
    • 五月五日曝薦席 - Means 'on the fifth day of the fifth month, she was airing out a mat.'
    • 忽見其三歲女在席上臥 - Means 'suddenly saw her three-year-old daughter lying on the mat.'
    • 驚怛便滅 - Means 'was shocked and disappeared.'
    • 女真形在別床如故 - Means 'the daughter's true form was still on another bed as usual.'
    • 不旬日而夭 - Means 'died within ten days.'
    • 世傳仲夏忌移床 - Means 'it is said that in midsummer, moving beds is taboo.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 謝晦 - Historical figure, a prominent official during the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 豫章 - Ancient name for Nanchang, the capital of Jiangxi province.
    • 胡充 - Historical figure, likely a local official or landowner.
    • 新野 - Ancient name for Xinye, a city in Henan province.
    • 庾 - Historical figure, likely a local official or landowner.
    • 仲夏 - Refers to the middle of summer, specifically the fifth month of the lunar calendar.

    Proper Nouns

    • 謝晦 - Historical figure, a prominent official during the Liu Song dynasty.
    • 豫章 - Ancient name for Nanchang, the capital of Jiangxi province.
    • 胡充 - Historical figure, likely a local official or landowner.
    • 新野 - Ancient name for Xinye, a city in Henan province.
    • 庾 - Historical figure, likely a local official or landowner.
    • 仲夏 - Middle of summer, specifically the fifth month of the lunar calendar.

    Chapter Five

    In the time of Qin, there was a temple of Lady Mei near the lake in DanYang County. Lady Mei, while alive, had magical powers and could walk on water with her shoes. Later, she betrayed the Daoist principles, and her husband, angered by this, killed her and threw her body into the water. Her body was carried by the waves until it reached the site of the temple. The temple's shamans instructed that her body should not be buried but placed in a coffin. Soon after, a coffin with a square head appeared under the temple. On the first and last days of each month, people could see the shape of her shoes in the water mist. The area around the temple was forbidden for fishing and hunting, as those who violated this rule would suffer from getting lost and drowning.

    The Palace Temple on the Lake is very powerful and effective. Merchants and travelers passing by, if they pray, can immediately summon a division of the lake, allowing those traveling upstream or downstream to sail smoothly without any danger.

    In the fifth year of the Yuanjia era, during the autumn evening, Hu Chong from Yuzhang had a large centipede, about three feet long, which fell in front of his wife and sister. He ordered the maid to throw it away. As soon as the maid stepped out of the door, she suddenly saw an old woman with smelly and rotten clothes and no eyes. By the third month of the sixth year, the entire family suffered from illnesses, dying one after another.

    Mao, the wife of Yu Zhi from Xinye, was airing out a mat on the fifth day of the fifth month. Suddenly, she saw her three-year-old daughter lying on the mat. She was shocked and the image disappeared. The daughter's true form was still on another bed as usual. Within ten days, the daughter died. It is said that in midsummer, moving beds is taboo. [39]

    秦时中宿县十里外有观亭江神祠，坛甚灵异。经过有不恪者，必狂走入山，变为虎。晋中朝有质子将归洛，反路见一行旅，寄其书云：“吾家在观亭，亭庙前石间有悬藤，即是也。君至，但扣藤，自有应者。”及归如言，果有二人从水中出，取书而没。寻还云：“河伯欲见君。”此人亦不觉随去，便睹屋宇精丽，饮食鲜香，言语接对，无异世间。今俗咸言观亭有江伯神也。
    　　汉武帝时，夜郎竹王神者名兴初，有女子浣于豚水，见三节大竹流入足间，推之不去。闻其中有号声，持破之，得一男儿。及长，有才武，遂雄夷獠氏，自立为夜郎侯，以竹为姓。所破之竹弃之于野，即生成林。王尝从人止石上，命作羹，从者曰：“无水。”王以剑击石泉，便涌出。今竹王水及破竹成林并存。后汉使唐蒙开郡，斩竹王首，夷獠咸诉以竹王非血气所生，甚重之，求为立后。太守吴霸以闻。帝封三子为侯，死配食父庙。今夜郎县有竹王三郎祠，是其神也。 [40]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [40]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 坛甚灵异 - The altar is very miraculous.
    • 经过有不恪者，必狂走入山，变为虎 - Those who pass by without respect will go mad and run into the mountains, transforming into tigers.
    • 君至，但扣藤，自有应者 - When you arrive, just tap the vine, and there will be a response.
    • 此人亦不觉随去，便睹屋宇精丽，饮食鲜香，言语接对，无异世间 - This person did not realize he was following them and soon saw magnificent buildings, fragrant and delicious food, and conversations that were no different from those in the world.
    • 王尝从人止石上，命作羹，从者曰：“无水。”王以剑击石泉，便涌出 - The king once asked someone to stop on a rock and ordered them to make soup. The follower said, 'There is no water.' The king struck the rock with his sword, and water gushed out.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 秦时中宿县 - Refers to a specific time and place during the Qin Dynasty.
    • 晋中朝 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty, specifically the central government.
    • 汉武帝时 - Refers to the reign of Emperor Wu of Han.
    • 夜郎竹王 - Refers to a legendary figure in the history of the Yi and Lao tribes.
    • 竹王 - Refers to a deity or legendary figure associated with bamboo.
    • 夷獠 - Refers to the Yi and Lao tribes, ethnic groups in ancient China.

    Proper Nouns

    • 秦 - The Qin Dynasty.
    • 晋 - The Jin Dynasty.
    • 汉武帝 - Emperor Wu of Han.
    • 夜郎 - A historical region in southern China.
    • 竹王 - The Bamboo King, a legendary figure.
    • 夷獠 - The Yi and Lao tribes.

    During the Qin Dynasty, in the middle of Xuzhou County, ten li away from the county, there was a temple of the River God at Guan Ting. The altar was very miraculous. Those who passed by without respect would go mad and run into the mountains, transforming into tigers. During the Jin Dynasty, a hostage prince was returning to Luoyang and on the return journey met a group of travelers, who entrusted a letter to him saying: "My home is at Guan Ting, where there is a hanging vine between the stones in front of the temple, that is it. When you arrive, just tap the vine, and there will be a response." When he returned as instructed, indeed two people emerged from the water, took the letter, and then disappeared. They soon returned and said, "The River God wishes to see you." This person did not realize he was following them and soon saw magnificent buildings, fragrant and delicious food, and conversations that were no different from those in the world. Nowadays, people generally say that there is a River God at Guan Ting.

    During the reign of Emperor Wu of Han, the Night郎 Bamboo King, whose name was Xingchu, was a deity. A woman washing in the Tún River saw a three-jointed large bamboo pole flow into her feet and could not push it away. She heard a cry from inside and broke it open, finding a boy. When he grew up, he was talented and brave, and became the leader of the Yi and Lao tribes, declaring himself the Night郎 Lord and taking the surname Bamboo. The bamboo pole that was broken was left in the wild and immediately grew into a forest. The king once asked someone to stop on a rock and ordered them to make soup. The follower said, "There is no water." The king struck the rock with his sword, and water gushed out. Nowadays, the Bamboo King Water and the forest of broken bamboo still exist. Later, the Han envoy Tang Meng opened the  Prefecture and cut off the head of the Bamboo King. The Yi and Lao people all appealed, saying that the Bamboo King was not born of flesh and blood, and they highly respected him, requesting to establish a successor. The emperor enfeoffed his three sons as marquises, and after their deaths, they were offered sacrifices at their father's temple. Nowadays, there is a Three-Lang Bamboo King Temple in the Night郎 County, which is his deity. [40]

    吴郡桐庐有徐君庙，吴时所立。左右有为劫盗、非法者，便如拘缚，终致讨执。东阳长山县吏李舀，义熙中遭事在郡，妇出料理，过庙请乞恩，拔银钗为愿。未至富阳，有白鱼跳落妇前。剖腹，得所愿钗，夫事寻散。
    　　晋永嘉中，吴相伍员庙，吴郡人叔父为台郎在洛，值京都倾废，归途阻塞。当济江，南风不得进。既投奏，即日得渡。
    　　陶侃曾如厕，见数十人悉持大印，有一人朱衣、平上帻，自称后帝，云以君长者，故来相报。三载勿言，富贵至极。侃便起，旋失所在，有大印作公字当其秽处，杂五行书，曰厕神、曰后帝。
    　　侃家童千余人，尝得胡奴，不喜言，尝默坐。侃一日出郊，奴执鞭以随，胡僧见而惊礼，云此海山使者也。侃异之，至夜失奴所在。 [41]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [41]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 为之一空 - Set phrase meaning completely emptied; fields were left barren.
    • 便如拘缚，终致讨执 - Metaphorical expression describing the immediate and inevitable consequences of actions.
    • 未至富阳，有白鱼跳落妇前，剖腹，得所愿钗 - Describes a sudden and unexpected event followed by a ritual act of making a vow.
    • 既投奏，即日得渡 - Literal description of crossing the river after submitting a petition.
    • 陶侃曾如厕，见数十人悉持大印 - Euphemism for going to the toilet and a metaphorical description of seeing people holding large seals.
    • 云以君长者，故来相报 - Metaphorical language describing the encounter with a virtuous person.
    • 三载勿言，富贵至极 - Metaphorical advice to remain silent for three years to achieve wealth and honor.
    • 侃便起，旋失所在 - Literal description of getting up and losing sight of something.
    • 有大印作公字当其秽处 - Literal description of a large seal with the character 'gong' (public) in the dirty place.
    • 杂五行书，曰厕神、曰后帝 - Reference to various writings in the Five Elements, calling it the Toilet God and the Emperor.

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 徐君庙 - The Xu Jun Temple, dedicated to Xu Jun, a historical figure.
    • 义熙中 - The Yihexi period, a specific era in Chinese history.
    • 吴相伍员庙 - The Wu Xiang Wu Yuan Temple, dedicated to Wu Yuan, a high-ranking official in the Wu state.
    • 台郎 - A government official in the imperial court.
    • 京都倾废 - The collapse of the capital, referring to a historical event.
    • 陶侃 - Cao Kan, a historical figure known for his integrity and virtue.
    • 厕神 - The Toilet God, a deity associated with toilets in Chinese folklore.
    • 后帝 - The Emperor, a title given to a ruler.

    Proper Nouns

    • 徐君庙 - The Xu Jun Temple.
    • 李舀 - Li, a proper noun.
    • 义熙中 - The Yihexi period.
    • 吴相伍员庙 - The Wu Xiang Wu Yuan Temple.
    • 台郎 - A government official.
    • 京都 - The capital.
    • 陶侃 - Cao Kan, a historical figure.
    • 厕神 - The Toilet God.
    • 后帝 - The Emperor.

    In Wu Prefecture's Tonglu, there is the Xu Jun Temple, established during the Wu period. Those who commit robbery and illegal acts in the vicinity will be bound as if by shackles and ultimately caught. An official Li from Changshan County in Dongyang was involved in an incident during the Yihexi period in the prefecture. His wife went out to handle matters and passed by the temple to ask for favors, pulling out her silver hairpin as a vow. Before reaching Fuyang, a white fish jumped in front of her. When she cut open the fish, she found the silver hairpin she had vowed for, and her husband's matter was eventually resolved.

    During the Yongjia period of the Jin dynasty, there was the Wu Xiang Wu Yuan Temple in Wu Prefecture. A man from Wu Prefecture, his uncle was a Tai Lang in Luoyang, during the collapse of the capital, the return journey was blocked. When he was about to cross the river, the south wind prevented him from proceeding. After submitting a petition, he crossed the river that very day.

    Cao Kan once went to the toilet and saw dozens of people all holding large seals. One man wore red clothes and a flat-topped hat, calling himself the Emperor. He said that because you are a virtuous person, he came to report to you. Do not speak for three years, and you will reach the highest level of wealth and honor. Cao Kan got up, and soon lost sight of them. There was a large seal with the character 'gong' (public) in the dirty place, and there were various writings in the Five Elements, calling it the Toilet God and the Emperor.

    Cao Kan had over a thousand servants, and once he acquired a foreign slave who did not like to speak and often sat silently. One day, Cao Kan went out to the countryside, and the slave held a whip to follow him. A foreign monk saw him and was surprised, treating him with great respect, saying that he was the messenger of the sea and mountains. Cao Kan was amazed by this, and by night, the slave had disappeared. [41]

    晋丹阳县有袁双庙，真第四子也。真为桓宣武所诛，便失所在。灵怪太元中形见于丹阳，求立庙。未既就功，大有虎灾，被害之家，辄梦双至催功甚急。百姓立祠堂，于是猛暴用息。今道俗常以二月晦鼓舞祈祠，尔日风雨忽至。元嘉五年，设奠讫，村人邱都于庙后见一物，人面龟身，葛巾七孔，端正而有酒气，未知双之神为是物凭也。
    　　青溪小姑庙，云是蒋侯第三妹庙，中有大扶疏，鸟尝产育其上。晋太元中，陈郡谢庆执弹乘马，缴杀数头，即觉体中栗然。至夜，梦一女子衣裳楚楚，怒云：“此鸟是我所养，何故见侵。”经日谢卒。庆名奂，灵运父也。
    　　余杭县有仇王庙，由来多神异。晋隆安初，县人树伯道为吏，得假将归，于汝南湾觅载，见一朱舸，中有贵人，因求寄。须臾，如睡，犹闻有声，若剧甚雨。俄而至家，以问船工，亦云仇王也。伯道拜谢而还。 [42]

    Relevant Explanation

    Explanation

    [42]

    Idiomatic Expressions

    • 真第四子也 - Indicates that '真' is the fourth son of someone, though the parent is not specified.
    • 靈怪太元中形見於丹陽 - '靈怪' refers to supernatural phenomena, and '太元中' is the reign title of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (376-396 AD). '形見' means 'to appear in form.'
    • 求立廟，未既就功，大有虎災 - Means 'to request the establishment of a temple,' 'before the project was completed,' and 'a significant occurrence of tiger attacks.'
    • 被害之家，辄梦双至催功甚急 - '辄梦' means 'would always dream,' and '催功甚急' means 'to urge the work very urgently.'
    • 百姓立祠堂，于是猛暴用息 - '用' here means 'thus' or 'thereby,' and '息' means 'to stop' or 'to cease.'
    • 今道俗常以二月晦鼓舞祈祠 - '道俗' refers to both monks and laypeople, and '二月晦' means 'the last day of the second month.'
    • 尔日风雨忽至 - '尔日' means 'on that day,' and '风雨忽至' means 'suddenly the wind and rain came.'
    • 元嘉五年，设奠讫，村人邱都于庙后见一物 - '元嘉五年' is the fifth year of the reign of Emperor Wu of Liu Song (428 AD), and '设奠讫' means 'after the offering was set up.'
    • 人面龟身，葛巾七孔，端正而有酒气 - Describes a creature with a human face, turtle body, and a headscarf with seven holes. '端正' means 'upright' or 'well-proportioned,' and '有酒气' means 'with the smell of alcohol.'
    • 未知双之神为是物凭也 - '未知' means 'unknown,' '双之神' refers to 'the spirit of Double,' and '为是物凭也' means 'whether it was this creature that possessed it.'
    • 青溪小姑庙，云是蒋侯第三妹庙 - '青溪' is a place name, '小姑' means 'young maiden,' and '蒋侯第三妹' means 'the third sister of Prince Jiang.'
    • 中有大扶疏，鸟尝产育其上 - '大' likely refers to a large tree or plant, and '扶疏' means 'spreading branches.' '尝' means 'often' or 'frequently,' and '产育' means 'to give birth' or 'to nurture.'
    • 晋太元中，陈郡谢庆执弹乘马，缴杀数头 - '晋太元中' is the same reign title as mentioned earlier, '陈郡谢庆' is a proper name, '执弹乘马' means 'holding a bow and riding a horse,' and '缴杀数头' means 'killed several birds with a bowstring.'
    • 即觉体中栗然，至夜，梦一女子衣裳楚楚 - '即觉' means 'immediately felt,' and '体中栗然' means 'a chill or shiver through the body.' '至夜' means 'at night,' and '衣裳楚楚' means 'dressed beautifully.'
    • 怒云：“此鸟是我所养，何故见侵。”经日谢卒 - '怒云' means 'angrily said,' and '何故见侵' means 'why do you harm them.' '经日' means 'after several days,' and '谢卒' means 'Xie died.'
    • 庆名奂，灵运父也 - '庆名奂' means 'Xie's name was Huan,' and '灵运父也' means 'he was the father of Xie Lingyun.'
    • 余杭县有仇王庙，由来多神异 - '余杭县' is a place name, '仇王庙' means 'the temple of King Chou,' and '由来多神异' means 'has long been known for its many supernatural phenomena.'
    • 晋隆安初，县人树伯道为吏 - '晋隆安初' is the early years of the reign of Emperor An of Jin (397-402 AD), and '县人树伯道为吏' means 'a county official named Shu Bodao.'
    • 得假将归，于汝南湾觅载 - '得假将归' means 'got leave to return home,' and '于汝南湾觅载' means 'sought a ride at Ruinan Bay.'
    • 见一朱舸，中有贵人 - '朱舸' means 'a red boat,' and '中有贵人' means 'there was a noble person inside.'
    • 因求寄，须臾，如睡，犹闻有声，若剧甚雨 - '因求寄' means 'asked to be carried along.' '须臾' means 'a short while,' '如睡' means 'as if asleep,' '犹闻有声' means 'still heard sounds,' and '若剧甚雨' means 'like heavy rain.'
    • 俄而至家，以问船工，亦云仇王也 - '俄而' means 'soon,' '至家' means 'arrived home,' '以问船工' means 'asked the boatman,' and '亦云仇王也' means 'also said it was King Chou.'
    • 伯道拜谢而还 - '拜谢' means 'bowed in gratitude,' and '而还' means 'and returned.'

    Cultural and Historical Allusions

    • 晋丹阳县 - A place name in the Jin Dynasty.
    • 桓宣武 - Refers to Huan Xuanwu, a prominent figure in the Jin Dynasty.
    • 太元中 - The reign title of Emperor Xiaowu of Jin (376-396 AD).
    • 元嘉五年 - The fifth year of the reign of Emperor Wu of Liu Song (428 AD).
    • 晋隆安初 - The early years of the reign of Emperor An of Jin (397-402 AD).
    • 青溪小姑庙 - A temple dedicated to a young maiden, possibly a local deity.
    • 蒋侯第三妹 - The third sister of Prince Jiang, a historical figure.
    • 余杭县 - A place name in the Jin Dynasty.
    • 仇王庙 - A temple dedicated to King Chou, a historical or legendary figure.
    • 汝南湾 - A place name where the story takes place.

    Syntactic Ambiguities

    • The sentence structure is straightforward but requires context to understand the relationship.
    • The sentence is clear but requires understanding of the time period and the nature of the supernatural phenomena.
    • The sequence of events is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the logical flow.
    • The sentence structure is clear but requires understanding of the supernatural elements.
    • The sentence is clear but requires maintaining the cause-and-effect relationship.
    • The sentence is clear but requires understanding of the ritual practices.
    • The sentence is clear but requires maintaining the suddenness of the event.
    • The sequence of events is clear but requires careful translation to maintain the narrative flow.
    • The description is clear but requires maintaining the vivid imagery.
    • The sentence is clear but requires understanding of the supernatural elements.
    • The sentence is clear but requires understanding of the historical context.
    • The sentence is clear but requires maintaining the vivid imagery.
    • The sentence is clear but requires understanding of the historical context.
